framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link — the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.what_it_is: Regularly turning raw freight and logistics market data (rates, capacity, demand signals) into public, data-backed commentary through a weekly update, newsletter, or research brief.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you can turn messy market data into judgment other practitioners are willing to act on — trust with ambiguity that a dashboard alone can't demonstrate.

paths.what_it_is: Building rigorous, math-based models (network design, inventory placement, risk mitigation) that solve real supply chain trade-offs, then publishing or teaching the methodology openly.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves your recommendations rest on quantitative rigor that can be checked and reused, not intuition alone — the confidence a sponsor needs to bet on your judgment.

paths.path: Procurement and sourcing public thought leadership
paths.what_it_is: Producing a long-running public body of work — a podcast, blog, or newsletter — that dissects real strategic sourcing and procurement decisions for a practitioner audience.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves your sourcing judgment holds up in the open over time, not just inside one company's negotiations — range that scales past a single employer.
